ops.title: getaway v2: preferences never gate; budgeted expansion
ops.body:
# getaway v2 rulings: preferences never gate; budgeted expansion

Supersedes "Getaway skill redesign: balance-bias, seat quality ranking, transfer-first affordability" (98be9f3). The balance-bias posture generalized in v2 from a rule about balances into the engine's core law.

## Preferences never gate — feasibility, confirmed constraints, and vetoes may

The complete hard set: structural journey validity (ordered, non-overlapping segments; return departs after outbound arrives), known seat insufficiency (`seat_sufficiency: insufficient`, judged on the live-expanded `/trips/{id}` row — never the cached `/search` teaser; `unknown` stays visible with a verification warning), endpoint + transit vetoes, hard-avoid carriers on any expanded segment, and explicitly confirmed `constraints`. Everything else — dates, trip length, departure weekdays, cabin, mileage target, layover style, program preference, balances, elite status, seat verdicts — orders or annotates through CLI-computed fit facts and tier verdicts, never removes. A journey beyond the preferred window composes and carries its named miss; the renderer, not the model, guarantees `preference_misses` display.

## Soft means swept wider, not filtered later

Soft dates sweep the stated range plus `SOFT_DATE_SEARCH_PADDING_DAYS` (7) — padding independent of preference strength; confirmed-hard dates sweep exact bounds. All cabins ride one call; `include_filtered=true` so the server can't hide expensive near-misses; sources are never trimmed to preferences. A miss must exist in the cache before the model can judge it.

## Expansion is budgeted per endpoint, disclosed when it truncates

`EXPANSION_BUDGET_PER_ENDPOINT` / `RETURN_EXPANSION_BUDGET_PER_ENDPOINT` (12 each), selection cohort-diversified over (endpoint, date, program, cost), truncation always disclosed. No global cap; one hot endpoint can't starve the others. `PRESENTATION_LIMIT` (6) applies only after assess, which additionally selects up to 2 notable preference stretches from beyond the cut.

## Surviving rulings, carried forward unchanged

- **`barely` demotes in-band**: a `barely` seat verdict soft-demotes within its cost tier, keeps its spot, and carries an explicit warning; unknown products rank neutral.
- **QAF first for Africa**: Africa sweeps lead with the undocumented `QAF` pseudo-code; per-program continent fallbacks sweep all programs funded-first, never trimmed.
- **Pseudo-codes never reach filters** (new, from the v1 zero-finalists bug): `WST`/`QAF` are sweep-time operands the server expands; every feasibility check compares the expanded concrete airports recorded on the leg.
ops.when: Before modifying retrieval, shortlist, expansion budgets, preference/constraint handling, or anything that filters rows in the getaway CLI.
